rationale: The page provides a structured overview of the Natyashastra, including its origins, core concepts, treatise details, Rasa theory, and its influence on living dance traditions. It is a high-quality educational summary of a key text in Indian culture.
cleanedText:
The Natyashastra: Foundation of Indian Performing Arts
1. Ancient Origins
Attributed to sage Bharata, composed circa 2nd century BCE
2. Core Concepts
Drama (natya), expression (abhinaya), music (sangita), emotion (bhava)
3. Comprehensive Treatise
Contains approximately 36,000 verses detailing every aspect of performance
4. Rasa Theory
Introduced the revolutionary concept of aesthetic flavour and emotional essence
Living Traditions
- Bharatanatyam
- Kathak
- Kathakali
- Odissi
- Kuchipudi
Recognised globally as a seminal text on theatre, dance, and music — its influence extends far beyond India's borders.
